title: Ultima zi (sper) fără o ministră sau un ministru al educației
summary: The author, an education advocate, reflects on the eve of a new education minister's appointment, criticizing the coalition's failed reforms and rector-dominated negotiations that ignored pre-university education. She calls for depoliticization of education and a meaningful Education Pact backed by an independent mechanism with real experts.
talking_points: The coalition's education negotiations were dominated by university rectors seeking more autonomy, while pre-university education was ignored
Summer non-reforms aged the teaching corps dramatically, increased simultaneous teaching class sizes, and demotivated students and experienced teachers who saw salary cuts
The current coalition represents a window of opportunity for a real Education Pact, but only if backed by a functional and independent mechanism staffed with experts
Electoral campaigns are no longer won by mobilizing teachers in party-colored jackets but through TikTok, making this the right time for depoliticization
Poverty deepens without proper education reform, and poverty is the enemy of education
author: Corina Atanasiu
